purpose: Hyperlane warp route for MXNB between Avalanche Fuji and Arc Testnet. Substitutes the real Bitso MXNB on Fuji (no public-mint faucet, keeper is not a minter) with a we-control TestnetFiatToken clone so the warp lane can be exercised end-to-end pre-Bitso handoff.

arc.deliveryRelayer: 0x0646FFe11b9aBcE0054Ce6F73025F06F3E91eC69 (keeper self-relayed; trustedRelayerIsm permits keeper, public Hyperlane relayer not whitelisted)

notes: Fuji token is a we-control TestnetFiatToken clone mirroring the Arc tMXNB at 0xe8F76f90553F50E76731afbeF1ac83a9152fFBEb (same Solidity, same name/symbol/decimals).

Production targets the real Bitso MXNB at 0xAB99d44185af87AeB08361588F00F59B0CE85eBb on Fuji. That token has no public-mint faucet and the keeper is not a minter, so the M2 lane uses the clone until Bitso onboards.
DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE + MINTER_ROLE on the Fuji clone are held by the keeper (0x0646...eC69) — same admin posture as the Arc tMXNB. Pending Bitso handoff: rotate admin to a Bitso-controlled multisig.

The Arc mailbox uses a trustedRelayerIsm whose relayer is the keeper. The public Hyperlane relayer therefore cannot deliver to Arc; the keeper self-relays via mailbox.process(metadata=0x, message). Same pattern is used for all current fx-telarana cross-chain delivery on Arc.
